title: rhus aromatica subsp. trilobata
intro: Rhus aromatica subsp. trilobata, also known as Skunkbush or New Mexico Locust, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for various medicinal purposes. Traditionally, the roots and leaves of Rhus aromatica subsp. trilobata have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, offering relief and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote digestive health.
